fields.name: * Collateral ID
fields.question:
Extract the Collateral ID (typically VIN, Vehicle Identification Number, Vehicle Serial Number) from the document and return only the VIN string exactly as it appears in the document. Follow these rules strictly:

A. What to capture:
Look for terms like: VIN, Vehicle ID, Vehicle Identification Number, Vehicle Serial Number, or any similar terms that might label a VIN.
Extract the VIN exactly as it appears in the document. Do not normalize, autocorrect, or retype any characters.
Do not substitute similar-looking characters: Do not convert 0 ↔ O, 1 ↔ I/L, 2 ↔ Z, 5 ↔ S, or 8 ↔ B. Keep characters exactly as shown.
A valid VIN is typically 17 alphanumeric characters and must not contain the letters I, O, or Q.
If the labeled string does not meet the 17-character requirement, or contains the letters I, O, or Q, do not extract it. Instead, return blank.

B. What to exclude:
Do not extract unrelated numbers, such as loan numbers, policy numbers, generic IDs, or sequences like 0000000000123.
If the value is not clearly labeled as a VIN or related term, do not extract.

C. Formatting and Validity Check:
The VIN should follow an alphanumeric format: e.g., ABCDE4DF3G2456742.
If "O" is detected convert to "0"
If "I" is detected convert to "1"
If "Q" is detected convert to "0"

D. Output:
Return only the VIN string exactly as shown in the document. Do not return any labels, descriptions, or extra text.
If no valid VIN is present, or if the VIN does not meet the required conditions (17 characters, no I, O, Q), return blank.

E. Final validation before output:
Only return the VIN if it is a valid 17-character VIN (with no I, O, or Q).
If the VIN does not meet these criteria, return blank.

fields.name: * Collateral Make
fields.question: Identify and extract only the vehicle make name from the given document exactly as it appears in the source, without any alteration. Preserve the original text exactly — do not expand abbreviations (e.g., “Cad” must not become “Cadillac”, “volks” must not become “Volkswagen”), do not change case, do not reformat, and do not correct spelling. Ignore the model, year, trim, or any additional details. Return only the make name with no extra words, labels, or inferred content. If the make is shown as an abbreviation, output it exactly as shown. If no make is present, return a blank result.

fields.name: * Collateral Model
fields.question: Extract the vehicle model from given vehicle information if not found then return blank.

fields.name: * Collateral Year
fields.question:
Extract the year of the collateral (vehicle) from the document according to the following rules:
Exact preservation: Extract the year value exactly as it appears in the document.
Format: The year may be in 2-digit or 4-digit format.
If it appears as 2 digits, return the 2 digits.
If it appears as 4 digits, return the 4 digits.
No alteration: Do not alter, convert, or expand the year format. Preserve it exactly.
Scope: Focus only on the section of the document that explicitly refers to the collateral and its associated year, ensuring that the year is clearly linked to collateral details.

Exclusions:
Do not extract years from unrelated dates such as policy effective dates, change dates, or processing dates.
Do not extract numbers from model codes, VIN fragments, or serial numbers that may look like years.
No inference: Do not infer, assume, or derive the collateral year from the VIN, model information, or any other context. Extract only if the year is explicitly stated in the document.
If not found: Return blank if no valid collateral year is present.

fields.name: * Lienholder
fields.question:
Extract the full name of the Lienholder or Loss Payee from the document. The name should be presented exactly as it appears in the document, without any transformation. Focus on sections that mention keywords such as 'Loss Payee,' 'Additional Interest,' 'Lienholder,' or 'Payee.' This name is typically that of the financial institution, such as a Credit Union or Financial Credit Union, providing the loan for the vehicle purchase. Ensure the extracted name reflects the full name of the institution, including any specific terms like 'Federal Credit Union' or 'Bank.'

If no Lienholder or Loss Payee name is found, return blank. Do not capture any other name if a Lienholder name is not present in the document.

fields.name: Carrier Agent Email
fields.question: Extract exactly one email address only if it is explicitly tied to the policy’s servicing contacts and passes all of these constraints: the email must appear on the same line, within the same table cell, or within two lines of a positive anchor label for the policy’s servicing party such as “Agent,” “Agency,” “Producer,” “Agent Contact,” “Serviced By,” “Insurance Company,” “Insurer,” “Carrier,” or “Underwriting Company,” and it must not be associated with any other roles (e.g., “Lienholder,” “Loss Payee,” “Credit Union,” “Insured,” “Policyholder,” “Mortgagee”). Reject any email that appears in headers/footers or near phrases indicating enrollment, marketing, or mailing logistics, including (case-insensitive) “Interested in receiving notices electronically,” “Electronic Service Requested,” “enroll,” “e-notice,” “paperless,” “mailing,” “certificate of mailing,” “for questions call,” “contact us,” “customer service,” or “support.” Explicitly exclude any address at the @dxc.com domain (e.g., incs.ops@dxc.com) and any email on the same line as those e-notice phrases, even if an anchor word is nearby. Do not return emails belonging to lienholders, policyholders, lenders, brokers not acting as the servicing agent of record, or third-party vendors. The email must be in a valid local@domain format and must be returned exactly as it appears without alteration. If no email satisfies the positive-anchor rule and all exclusions, return blank.

fields.name: Carrier Agent Fax
fields.question:
Extract the Carrier Agent Fax number according to the following rules:
Priority 1: Extract the FAX number of the agent or producer.
Priority 2: If no agent or producer FAX number is found, extract the FAX number of the insurance company or carrier.
Association requirement: Return the FAX number only if it is explicitly associated with the agent, producer, insurance company, or carrier.

Exclusions: Do not extract any FAX number associated with “credit union”, “lienholder”, “policy holder”, or any other unrelated party.
If not found: Return blank if no fax number is found.
Formatting: Extract the fax number exactly as it appears in the document, preserving all dots, spaces, parentheses, or dashes but removing any trailing or leading text labels such as “fax”, “fax:”, “(fax)”, or similar.

Multiple Numbers Rule:
If multiple fax numbers are attached together in a single field (e.g., separated by /, ,, ;, or |), return only the first fax number exactly as it appears, stopping before the delimiter.
Examples:
800 852 9001/800-852-9001 → 800 852 9001
(123) 456-7890, (987) 654-3210 → (123) 456-7890
1-800-555-0000; 1-800-555-1111 → 1-800-555-0000
888-222-3333 | 888-444-5555 → 888-222-3333

fields.name: Carrier Name
fields.value: Florida Farm Bureau Casualty® Insurance Company
fields.question:
Extract the Carrier Name from the document. Follow these rules strictly, and output only the carrier name exactly as it appears in the document (no extra words, no quotes, no labels).
Primary source = Logo/Image text
Identify company names from visible logos, mastheads, letterheads, watermarks, or branded marks on any page.
Prefer the parent company’s logo/name over an underwriting or subsidiary company’s name.
Logo cleanup: When extracting from a logo, exclude decorative marks, taglines, or special characters (™, ®, ©, “|”, “-”, etc.). Capture only the actual carrier name text.

AAA special case
If both "AAA" and Interinsurance Exchange of the Automobile Club appear, return AAA.
If AAA logo/name is not present but Interinsurance Exchange of the Automobile Club is, return Interinsurance Exchange of the Automobile Club exactly as shown.

Parent vs. subsidiary
When both a parent and a subsidiary/underwriting company are present, return the parent company’s name.
Textual fallbacks (only if no logo/brand mark found)
If no logo text is available, extract the full carrier name explicitly stated in the document.
Look for textual indicators such as: “Carrier,” “Insurer,” “Insurance Company,” “Company,” “Issued by,” “Underwritten by,” or header/footer branding.
Prefer the parent company’s name over underwriting entities when both appear.

No inference
Do not infer or guess the carrier from policy numbers, NAIC codes, URLs, emails, addresses, or context. Extract only names that explicitly appear in the document.

Exact preservation
Preserve the original casing, punctuation, accents, and spacing exactly as printed.
If multiple candidates remain
Choose the name tied to the most prominent brand presentation (e.g., top-of-page masthead or largest logo).
If still tied, choose the one appearing first in reading order.
If nothing valid is present
Return nothing (leave the output blank). Do not write placeholders or explanations.

alternateCandidates.value: Florida Farm Bureau Casualty Insurance Company
Florida Farm Bureau Casualty® Insurance Company

fields.name: Document Issue Date
fields.question:
Extract the document’s issue date from the actual policy document (not from fax or email cover pages). Follow these rules:
Primary Target: Look for labels such as “Issue Date”, “Printed Date”, “Date of Notice”, “Mailing Date”, “Document Created”, “Value Date”, “Entry Date”, “Processed Date”, “Produced”, or any equivalent indicators.
Acceptable Issue Dates: Also include dates labeled or implied as:
Billing date
Calendar date
Countersigned
Created
Current date
Date and time requested
Date of notice
Entry date
Generated date
Information as on date
Issued
Mailed
Online policy summary as of date
Prepared date
Printed
Procedural date
Processed date
Produced
Review date
Run on date
Signed
Unlabeled standalone date (if clearly associated with document issuance)
Website date
Transmit date
Statement date
Completed date
Unacceptable Issue Dates: Do not use dates labeled as or associated with:
Application date (unless explicitly tied to “New Business Renewal”)
Effective date
Fax date
Future dates (later than today’s date)
Inception date
Invoice date
Transaction effective date
Exclusions: Ignore dates appearing on fax/email headers or transmission metadata (e.g., “Fax”, “Email”, “To”, “From”, “Subject”). Also ignore dates that clearly correspond to policy coverage terms (Effective/Expiration/Renewal).
Selection Rule: If multiple qualifying dates are present, select the one most directly tied to issuance/production of the document, prioritizing those on the first page of the policy content.
Output Format: Return only the date in MM/DD/YYYY format.
If Nothing Found: Leave the output blank (no value).

fields.name: Loan Number
fields.question: Extract any number labelled as a "Loan Number". Do not extract the policy number or any other number where it's not specified as "loan number". Return blank if not found.

fields.name: Policy Holder Name
fields.question:
Extract the First Policy Holder Name from the document & Follow these rules strictly:
A. What to capture:
Look for keywords like: Insured Name, Owner, Policyholder, Insured, and Name: (especially in uppercase label formats).
Extract only the first policy holder’s name in case there are multiple names listed.
Example: If names are listed as William, Robert and Linda, only extract “William, Robert”.
Do not include connector words such as “&” or “and” in the extracted name. Ignore them completely.
Do not alter or modify the name in any way. Do not reformat or split first and last names.
Preserve the exact original case, spacing, punctuation, and special characters for the first policy holder only.
If the name includes multiple people, extract only the first name listed, excluding any connectors.
If the Policy Holder is a company name, extract the company name exactly as it appears (including any special characters).

B. Output format:
Return only the name (nothing else).
Do not include labels, notes, or anything apart from the name itself.

C. Examples of correct output:
William, Robert and Linda → “William, Robert”
ALEX KIM → “ALEX KIM”
MARIA KIM → “MARIA KIM”
ALEX and MARIA KIM → “ALEX”
ENRIQUE MORENO, LAURA MORENO → “ENRIQUE MORENO”
ENRIQUE MORENO and LAURA MORENO J → “ENRIQUE MORENO”
Company name: ACME INSURANCE, INC. → “ACME INSURANCE, INC.”
Insured Name: JOHN DOE → “JOHN DOE”
Name: XYZ DBA, ABCD → “XYZ DBA, ABCD”

D. No inferences or transformations:
Do not infer or guess names if not explicitly written.
Do not split, reorder, or assume formatting.
Example: John, Smith → “John, Smith” (not “Smith, John”).
If multiple names are present (e.g., John, Smith and Jane), extract only the first holder’s name (John, Smith).
If names are separated by commas, dashes, or other punctuation, return the first name exactly as written, excluding any following connectors like “&” or “and”.

fields.name: Policy Number
fields.question:
Extract the Policy Number from the given document, following these rules:

Return exactly as shown in the document – preserve casing, spaces, punctuation, prefixes, suffixes, and all leading/trailing zeros.
Formats allowed – numeric or alphanumeric, may include spaces, dashes, slashes, or letters (e.g., GG 000123-AX).
Primary source – Use the value that appears next to any of the following labels (case-insensitive, punctuation variants allowed): Policy Number, Policy No, Policy #, Policy ID, Policy, Policy/Number, or Policy:.
If multiple policy numbers appear and an update is stated (e.g., “policy number changed from X to Y”), return only the newer value.

Carrier-specific fallback rules – Only if no explicit policy number is found:
  - For The Hartford, use the Ref No. exactly as printed.
  - For Citizens Property Insurance, use the Certificate Number exactly as printed.

Do NOT capture claim numbers, account numbers, quote numbers, invoice numbers, form numbers, NAIC codes, producer/agent numbers, application numbers, VINs, FEINs, or dates.
If no valid value is found after applying the above rules, return nothing — leave the output completely blank, without any placeholder text or explanation.
Output only the policy number exactly as it appears in the document.

fields.name: Co Policy Holder Name
fields.question:
Extract the Co-Policy Holder Name from the document and output only the second name, exactly as it appears, following these rules:
A. What to capture:
Extract only the second policy holder listed.
If more than two names are listed (e.g., ALEX, MARIA, and JOHN SMITH), ignore the 3rd, 4th, etc. names — only capture the 2nd person’s name.
If names are separated by “and”, “&”, commas, or other punctuation, extract only the second person’s name — do not include the connectors themselves (“and” or “&”).
Do not alter or reformat the name in any way.
Preserve original case, spacing, punctuation, and any special characters exactly as they appear.
If only the first name is given for the second insured person, return only that (do not add or infer a surname).

B. Output format:
Return only the name of the second policy holder.
The output should not include any labels, notes, or anything apart from the name itself.

C. Examples of correct output:
ALEX and MARIA KIM → “MARIA KIM”
ALEX KIM and MARIA → “MARIA”
JOHN DOE and JANE SMITH → “JANE SMITH”
ENRIQUE MORENO, LAURA MORENO J, and JUAN PEREZ → “LAURA MORENO J”
ALEX, MARIA, JOHN → “MARIA”
LOOKOUT IRVIN, CHRISSIE & LOOKOUT, STEVEN → “LOOKOUT, STEVEN”

D. No inferences or transformations:
If only one policy holder is listed, return blank.
Do not guess or infer missing parts of a name.
Do not include the 3rd, 4th, or further names even if present.

E. Special cases:
If there’s no second name listed (i.e., only one insured person), return blank.

fields.name: Policy Identification Number
fields.question: Extract the Policy Identification Number only if the document clearly belongs to TRAVELERS (check for indicators such as the “TRAVELERS” logo, the header “THE TRAVELERS INSURANCE COMPANIES,”). Once confirmed, locate the field labeled as “Policy Identification Number”. Return the full value exactly as displayed. If no such field is found, or if the document is not from TRAVELERS, return blank.

fields.name: Document Type Field
fields.question:
**Task**:
        Classify the type of this document based on its content.
        
        **Instructions**:
        
        - Classify the document strictly using one of the following document types:

ID Card:
   - Keywords/indicators: ID, ID card, insurance card, identification card.
   - Structure resembles an insurance ID card.

Temporary:
   - Temporary documents that begin a policy.
   - Includes binders, applications, or temporary coverage notices.
   - Identifiable by title, application number, or signature.

Reinstatement:
   - Sent after a cancellation notice but restores coverage.
   - Keywords: acknowledgement of payment, disregard cancel notice, withdraw cancellation, rescission/recession of cancellation.

New Business Renewal:
   - Any declaration or statement of coverage.
   - Keywords/phrases: new business, Renew, renewal, declaration, Re-issues, Re-write, statement of coverage, certificate of insurance, evidence of insurance.
   - Can have multiple headings.

Policy Change:
   - Any document reflecting a change to the policy or interest removal/termination of third party's interest.
   - Keywords: amendment, endorsement, third party interest termination, interest termination, termination of loss payee interest, interest terminated. 
   - Do NOT classify "policy termination" or "Notice of Termination Lienholder" as a policy change.
   - Multiple vehicles are present on the document and one of the vehicle is 'Deleted/removed', classify as "Policy Change".
   - If the 'endorsement/amendment' date and the 'policy effective date' are the same classify it is a "New Business Renewal". Do not classify it as Policy Change.

Cancellation:
   - Actual policy cancellation (not intent, Lienholder Interest removals that contain a cancel reason (non payment, company cancelled, underwriting, policyholder request).
   - Keywords: Lienholder Notice of Termination, Notice of Termination Lienholder, suspension of policy, Auto Policy Cancellation, Cancellation notice for non-payment of premium, termination of policy, cancellation of policy, removal of policy, delete policy/vehicle.
   - Do NOT include "interest"-only cancellations unless the title clearly states "Notice of Termination Lienholder" or "Lienholder Notice of Termination".
   - If there is only 1 vehicle on the policy & if the vehicle was deleted/removed classify it as "Cancellation".

Invalid Document:
   - Any document not fitting the above categories.
   - Includes "intent to cancel" or unrelated/non-insurance documents.
        
        - Focus on the main policy/insurance content when determining document type
        - **IMPORTANT**: Always consider document titles, headers, and section names that indicate the document's 
primary purpose (e.g., "DECLARATION", "CANCELLATION NOTICE", "POLICY CHANGE", "RENEWAL", etc.)
        - Ignore only generic sections such as:
            - General notices or instructions to third parties (lienholders, banks, etc.)
            - Standard disclaimers or legal boilerplate language
            - Contact information and business hours
            - Website references or procedural instructions
            - Template language about endorsements or general policy terms
            - Administrative notifications that don't relate to the main document purpose
        
        - If the document doesn't belong to any of the specified document types, classify it as "Invalid Document"
        - Always return one of the above types as the classification result
        
        **Output Format**:
        Return the result in the following JSON format, with no additional explanation:
        ```json
        {"document_type": "Document Type"}
        ```

fields.name: Collateral Pivot Field
fields.question:
Extract all unique vehicle details from this document in the format: year-make-model-vin

Instructions:
1. Look for ALL types of motor vehicles including: cars, trucks, motorcycles, boats, RVs, trailers, ATVs, jet skis, and any other motorized vehicles
2. For each vehicle found, extract available information in this exact order: YEAR-MAKE-MODEL-VIN
3. If any field is missing, skip it but maintain the format (e.g., if no model: year-make-vin)
4. Separate each vehicle record with a semicolon (;)
5. Remove duplicate entries based on available information:
   - If two entries have the same year-make-model combination, prioritize the one WITH a VIN
   - If one entry is a subset of another (e.g., "2018-GMC-SIERRA" vs "2018-GMC-SIERRA 1500-3GTU2NEC8JG544335"), keep only the more complete entry
   - Only include unique vehicle records after removing duplicates and subsets
6. For VIN/identification numbers:
   - Cars/trucks: typically 17 characters
   - Boats: HIN (Hull Identification Number) may be 12-14 characters
   - Motorcycles/ATVs: may vary in length
   - Include any vehicle identification number regardless of length
7. Ignore non-vehicle items like parts, accessories, or equipment
8. If no vehicles are found, return empty string

Output format example:
2020-FORD-F150-1FTFW1E50LFA12345;2018-HONDA-CIVIC-19XFC2F59JE123456;2019-YAMAHA-YZ450F-1FMF00000123456;1995-SEARAY-240SUNDANCER-SERYB123J495

Extract only the vehicle information, no explanations or additional text. Return empty string if no vehicles found.
